inline_comments.message:
you ll always be depended on hard-coded latest compose, however you can do fetch the latest compose by using the command like:

some comment like: get latest docker compose released tag
COMPOSE_VERSION=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/docker/compose/releases/latest | grep 'tag_name' | cut -d\" -f4)

Then, you can try:
With this command, you also will be independen of binary type i.e if the system is not linux 64 bit OS but x86_64, then `uname -s`-`uname -m` ll automatically download relevant the binaries for the system that use CSIT for onap dmi.

curl -s -L https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/${COMPOSE_VERSION}/docker-compose-`uname -s`-`uname -m` > bin/docker-compose

As discussed offline , its a good suggestion.
But relying on the latest version always will not be deterministic. Features always gets added or removed from this active project and we might face surprising issues with this.
So I would suggest to have the version hard coded and when we have a requirement to upgrade we can analyze the version we want to use then which is free from vulnerabilities.
